strengths: 20+ years of diverse engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — comfortably exceeds the 15-year minimum
Current role at re:cinq includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) framework development, showing hands-on engagement with agentic AI tooling

Demonstrated entrepreneurial and builder instincts: founded GISCollective, maintains multiple open-source projects, and has given conference talks — aligns with the 'startup within a larger org' preferred profile
concerns: No advanced degree in an AI-related field; educational background is a BS in Computer Science and a Fine Arts degree, well below the preferred Master's or PhD
AI-specific experience is both shallow and very recent — limited to a few months of MCP tooling since April 2025, with no track record of recognized AI products or platforms at scale
No evidence of director-level advisory work, strategic customer engagement, or the cross-organizational influence expected of a principal-level AI strategist at a large cloud/AI company
response.justification: The candidate brings genuine engineering breadth and an entrepreneurial spirit built over two decades, but the profile is that of a skilled senior generalist, not a principal AI strategist. The AI experience is limited to a few months of MCP tooling work at a small firm, with no history of building or shipping recognized AI products at scale, no advanced AI credentials, and no evidence of the strategic advisory, executive-level influence, or cloud-platform strategy work this director-level IC role demands. The gap between current profile and role requirements is too wide to bridge at interview.
